body:
Postmortem — first-consumer adoption (Minoo) of `waaseyaa/groups` surfaced 4 distinct framework gaps, each closed in its own release:

- **alpha.148** → registry wiring missing from kernel
- **alpha.149** → optional `bundleEnumerator` with registry fallback (dual-wire → single-wire)
- **alpha.150** → `getQuery` registry forward + `FieldStorage` enum + universal-field registration on canonical `Group`
- **alpha.151** → monorepo-wide constraint tightening (179 constraints / 54 manifests) + CP005 lint rule

Core lesson: the framework's 6,448-test suite green-lit every release because the test harness didn't boot the kernel the way consumer apps do. Source-tree co-resolution masked every gap.

## Proposed structural fix

1. **Kernel-path integration test** in the framework that boots the real kernel end-to-end, registers a bundle field via `addBundleFields()`, and asserts the subtable materializes on disk. If that test had existed in the original extraction PR, the five-release chain would have been a single release.

2. **Packaged-form CI:** install the published alpha from Packagist into a minimal consumer app and run a smoke suite. Catches cross-package version drift before it ships. Same class of issue that drives RP003 false alarms (#1310): no test environment resembles a real consumer's installed tree.

## Acceptance

- New integration test under `tests/Integration/Kernel/` that boots `HttpKernelTestCase` (or equivalent), registers a bundle field against a canonical entity type, and asserts `{base}__{bundle}` exists in the DB.
- CI job that composer-installs the framework packages from Packagist into a minimal skeleton app and runs a smoke suite on migrations + one save/read round-trip.
title: Kernel-path integration test for extractions (postmortem: five-release adoption chain)

Context: `SqlSchemaHandler::deriveColumnSpec()` falls through for non-trivial field types. During Minoo adoption (PR waaseyaa/minoo#740), `text_long`, `uri`, and `entity_reference` hit the fallback path and landed as TEXT without explicit column hints.

Ask: map these three explicitly.

- `text_long` → LONGTEXT (MySQL) / TEXT (SQLite) with explicit length semantics documented in spec
- `uri` → VARCHAR(2048) default, overridable per-field
- `entity_reference` → INT UNSIGNED / INTEGER with FK hint to target entity's base table

Spec work: add a field-type → column-spec mapping table to `specs/field/column-derivation.md` so deviations are visible at review time.

Related: alpha.150 `FieldStorage` enum — this extends the same "explicit over implicit" thread.

body:
Context from Minoo PR #740 adoption:
1. `GroupsServiceProvider` auto-registers `group`/`group_type`
2. Consumer (Minoo) `AppServiceProvider` attempted to re-register `group`/`group_type`
3. Pre-alpha.151 behaviour: silent bundle-field smear into `_data`
4. Post-alpha.151 behaviour: hard error with unclear message

Two asks, one issue:

**A. Shadow-collision guard** — when `addBundleFields()` is called against a bundle whose subtable doesn't yet exist, emit `LoggerInterface::notice()` so CI against a real DB surfaces the missing-migration case before production.

**B. Duplicate-registration DX** — when a consumer registers an entity type whose id is already registered by a framework package, the exception message should name BOTH registrants:

> "Entity type 'group' is already registered by `Waaseyaa\Groups\GroupsServiceProvider`. Your registration in `<ConsumerServiceProvider>` is duplicate. If you were shadowing the canonical type, drop this registration."

Rationale: during Minoo adoption the silent-smear class hit twice. Loud, directed errors close the whole class.
title: Shadow-collision guard + clear duplicate-registration error

body:
## Context

The post-deploy step `verify-tag-parity` (RP003) has been false-alarming on the last four tag rollouts (alpha.146–.149). Tags were correctly published to Packagist and pullable via `composer show -a`, so alarms haven't blocked work — but each requires manual operator confirmation.

## What to investigate

- Detection logic in `verify-tag-parity` — what is being compared, and at what timing point?
- Packagist webhook propagation delay — is the check running before propagation completes?
- Is the parity check comparing the framework tag against split-deploy mirrors, where one (or more) is the slow source?

## Proposed fix sketch

1. **Add backoff** — retry the parity check up to N times with a short sleep, succeeding on first match.
2. **Reframe the check** — drop from blocking path if propagation should happen out-of-band.

Watch for recurrence on alpha.150 — if it false-alarms a fifth time, fix rather than live with.

labels.name: sprint-candidate
labels.description: Track 4 schema slice — recommended next sprint batch

title: deploy: RP003 verify-tag-parity false alarms on alpha.146-.149

body:
## Context

After PR #1307 introduces `FieldStorage::Data`, a consumer can wind up in a state where a field is registered with `stored: FieldStorage::Data` but a column for the field still exists on the base or subtable. New writes go to `_data` (correct per the hint); old data lives in the column. Reads currently resolve via column-existence check (`SqlEntityQuery::resolveField()`), so the column wins until the symmetric query-side fix lands. Even after that fix, the data in the lingering column is silently stale.

## Proposed diagnostic

Add to `HealthChecker`:

- Code: `COLUMN_DATA_STORAGE_DRIFT`, severity `warning`.
- Detection: for every entity type with a registered `FieldStorage::Data` field, query the schema for any column with that field's name on the base table or any registered bundle subtable. Emit a warning per occurrence with table + column + field.

Surfaces in `waaseyaa health` and `waaseyaa schema:check`.
title: operator-diagnostics: detect column-vs-data storage drift

body:
## Context

PR #1307 (alpha.150) introduced `FieldStorage::Data` and made the **save** path authoritative: `SqlEntityStorage::splitForStorage()` routes `Data`-stored values into `_data` even when a legacy column happens to exist. The **query** path is asymmetric — `SqlEntityQuery::resolveField()` still defers to `SchemaInterface::fieldExists()` for routing, so if a column lingers from before the storage hint was set, queries will read from the (stale) column while writes land in `_data`.

For the Group case shipped in alpha.150 this is benign (no legacy columns exist for `status`/`created_at`/`updated_at`). It becomes a foot-gun the moment any consumer adds the storage hint to a field that already has a column on disk.

## Proposed fix

`SqlEntityQuery::routeFields()` should consult the registry's `stored` hint and pass a per-field directive into `resolveField()` that forces `json_extract(_data, ...)` regardless of column existence. Mirrors the `getDataStoredCoreFieldNames()` logic on the storage side.

## Why deferred

Out of scope for the alpha.150 PR per maintainer direction — that PR was already bundling Bug C (registry forward) and Gap D (strict UnknownField regression). Symmetric handling is a single-side correction with no behavior change for fresh installs; tracked separately so the alpha.150 surface stays narrow.
title: entity-storage: symmetric query-side handling for FieldStorage::Data when legacy column exists

body:
## Current

`SqlSchemaHandler::deriveColumnSpec()` (`packages/entity-storage/src/SqlSchemaHandler.php`) translates a `FieldDefinition::getType()` to a column spec via:

```php
$spec = match (strtolower($field->getType())) {
    'string' => ['type' => 'varchar', 'length' => (int) ($settings['length'] ?? 255)],
    'text' => ['type' => 'text'],
    'integer', 'int' => ['type' => 'int'],
    'boolean', 'bool' => ['type' => 'boolean'],
    'float', 'decimal', 'numeric', 'number' => ['type' => 'float'],
    default => ['type' => 'text'],
};
```

The `default` arm silently absorbs any field type the match doesn't know about — it returns a bare `text` column with no error, no warning, no log line.

## Problem

Consumer field types in common use fall straight through to `default`:

- `text_long` → should be explicitly `text` (same outcome today, but not by intent)
- `uri` → should be explicitly `varchar` or `text` (URIs are bounded-length strings, not free-form text)
- `entity_reference` → should be explicitly `int` (foreign-key-shaped) or `varchar` depending on the target entity type's id key

The silent default is acceptable today because the outcome (a TEXT column) matches what `_data` JSON storage produced for these types previously. It becomes a real problem when:

- A consumer registers a field type hoping for the right column shape and quietly gets `text` instead.
- A consumer *typos* a field type (`'integr'` instead of `'integer'`) and gets a `text` column that silently accepts integer values as strings — the bug surfaces downstream as query-time coercion weirdness.

## Surfaced by

Minoo's adoption of `waaseyaa/groups` (consumer PR in progress). Of the 19 business-bundle fields Minoo registers via `EntityTypeManager::addBundleFields()`, 3 types land on the `default` arm: `text_long` (description), `uri` (url, booking_url), `entity_reference` (community_id, media_id). All work by accident — the TEXT column happens to hold what Minoo needs — but this is the second consumer (after `waaseyaa/groups` itself) using `deriveColumnSpec()` and already reveals coverage gaps.

See also the follow-up already logged in `docs/specs/extraction-log.md`:

> **Shared FieldDefinition → column mapper.** `SqlSchemaHandler::deriveColumnSpec()` ... should be promoted to a shared mapper once a second consumer needs it. Until then, keep it private to `SqlSchemaHandler`; premature extraction without a second caller would bake the current limited type set into a shared contract.

This issue is the narrow fix (add explicit arms); promotion to a shared mapper is a separate, larger decision still worth deferring.

## Proposed

Add explicit match arms before the `default`:

```php
'text_long' => ['type' => 'text'],
'uri' => ['type' => 'varchar', 'length' => (int) ($settings['length'] ?? 2048)],
'entity_reference' => ['type' => 'int'],   // when target id key is the common integer PK
```

Separately: turn the silent `default` into a logged warning (via `LoggerInterface` passed through from `SqlSchemaHandler` construction, default `NullLogger`) — an operator deploying with a typoed field type should see a signal at boot, not at query time.

## Scope

- `packages/entity-storage/src/SqlSchemaHandler.php` — add match arms, add optional logger dep, log on default.
- `packages/entity-storage/tests/Unit/SqlSchemaHandlerTest.php` — add cases for the three new types.
- No API break. Existing `text_long`/`uri`/`entity_reference` registrations keep the same storage outcome (text column); new registrations get intent-matching columns going forward.

## Non-goals

- Promoting `deriveColumnSpec()` to a shared public mapper — deferred per extraction-log.
- Richer type shapes (composite types, JSON-typed columns, enum constraints) — out of scope here.
- DBAL-level type coercion — this is at the schema-spec generation layer, not DBAL.

## Consumer impact

Zero for existing consumers. `waaseyaa/groups` itself has no `text_long`/`uri`/`entity_reference` fields (ships with zero bundle fields pre-registered). Minoo, once adopted, gains correct column shapes without code changes.

labels.name: sprint-candidate
labels.description: Track 4 schema slice — recommended next sprint batch

title: Explicit type mappings in SqlSchemaHandler::deriveColumnSpec() for text_long, uri, entity_reference

body:
Discovered during Minoo's adoption of `waaseyaa/groups` (consumer-side adoption PR opening shortly).

## Current

`CommunityScope` uses `is_a($entityType->getClass(), HasCommunityInterface::class, true)` to decide whether to apply tenancy filtering (`packages/entity-storage/src/Tenancy/CommunityScope.php`). Consumer code must implement the `HasCommunityInterface` marker on the entity class for tenancy to activate.

## Problem

Couples tenancy opt-in to entity class hierarchy. Consumers who adopt a framework-shipped entity type (like `Waaseyaa\Groups\Group`) can't extend it to add the marker — framework `Group` is `final`, and un-finaling for the sake of this marker is the wrong trade-off. Bundle-scoped fields exist specifically to avoid this kind of class-hierarchy coupling for consumer API.

## Proposed direction

Move tenancy opt-in to `EntityType` declaration or storage registration. Two shapes worth considering:

1. `tenancy: ['scope' => 'community']` key on `EntityType` — declarative, lives with the rest of the entity-type shape.
2. Separate `TenantScope` registration hook — e.g. `$container->tag('entity.tenant_scope', entityType: 'group', scope: CommunityScope::class)`. Decouples tenancy concerns from core entity-type declaration.

Either lets consumers opt into tenancy without touching the entity class.

## Scope

Framework-side refactor. No consumer API changes required beyond a migration path for existing `HasCommunityInterface` implementers — likely a deprecation cycle where both paths work, with the marker interface logged as deprecated.

## Consumer impact until this lands

Minoo's `Group` entity (currently `App\Entity\Group implements HasCommunityInterface`) will stop participating in community-scoped queries when Minoo switches to `Waaseyaa\Groups\Group::class` in its `EntityType` registration. Minoo has zero production callers of `getCommunityId()` / `setCommunityId()` today (all usages are in marker-assertion tests), so the practical regression is nil — but the scoping infrastructure is no longer wired for `group` until this lands.

## References

- `packages/entity-storage/src/Tenancy/CommunityScope.php` — current `is_a()` check.
- `packages/entity/src/Community/HasCommunityInterface.php` — the marker interface.
- `packages/entity/src/Community/HasCommunityTrait.php` — the 6-line trait.
- Minoo adoption PR: *(to be linked once open)*.
title: Move tenancy opt-in from HasCommunityInterface marker to EntityType/storage registration

body:
Follow-up from the #1297 review.

`HealthChecker::findOrphanSubtables()` queries `sqlite_master`, which is SQLite-only. On MySQL / PostgreSQL the `\Throwable` catch silently skips with an info log — operators lose `ORPHAN_BUNDLE_SUBTABLE` detection entirely on non-SQLite deployments.

**Acceptance criteria:**

- Portable table enumeration via DBAL `AbstractSchemaManager::listTableNames()` filtered by the `{base}__%` pattern, **or** dialect-specific paths:
  - MySQL: `information_schema.tables` WHERE `table_schema = DATABASE()` AND `table_name LIKE ? ESCAPE '\\'`
  - PostgreSQL: `information_schema.tables` WHERE `table_schema = current_schema()` AND `table_name LIKE ? ESCAPE '\\'`
  - SQLite: current `sqlite_master` path, retained.
- `ORPHAN_BUNDLE_SUBTABLE` emission identical across drivers.
- Test matrix must include at least one non-SQLite run (docker-compose gated behind env var is acceptable).

Until this lands, `docs/specs/operator-diagnostics.md` should state explicitly that orphan detection requires SQLite.

**Specs:** `docs/specs/operator-diagnostics.md`, `docs/specs/bundle-scoped-storage.md` §Drift diagnostic.
title: Portable ORPHAN_BUNDLE_SUBTABLE detection for MySQL and PostgreSQL

body:
Follow-up from the #1297 review.

Per `CLAUDE.md` §Layer Architecture, `packages/foundation` (layer 0) may only import from its own layer or lower. `HealthChecker` currently imports from layer 1:

- `Waaseyaa\Entity\EntityTypeInterface`
- `Waaseyaa\Entity\EntityTypeManagerInterface`
- `Waaseyaa\EntityStorage\SqlSchemaHandler`
- `Waaseyaa\Entity\Field\FieldDefinitionRegistryInterface` — added in #1297

The violation is pre-existing; #1297 extended the pattern rather than introducing it. The nullable-optional ctor parameter mitigates hard coupling but not import direction. The pattern will compound as more diagnostic surfaces arrive.

**Options:**

1. **Relocate `HealthChecker`** to a higher layer (diagnostic kernel / interface layer) and inject it down. Cleanest; touches wiring in `ConsoleKernel`.
2. **Define a foundation-layer `SchemaDescribable` interface** that `packages/entity` and `packages/entity-storage` implement, and have `HealthChecker` consume that. Inverts the dependency; introduces a new abstraction.
3. **Accept as kernel-adjacent and document the exemption.** `HealthChecker` is wired only from `ConsoleKernel` today — functionally close to a bootstrapper. Cheapest option; codifies the status quo.

Architectural debt that compounds silently. Pick a direction before the next diagnostic lands.

**Spec:** `docs/specs/operator-diagnostics.md`; `CLAUDE.md` §Layer Architecture.
title: Extract HealthChecker's entity/entity-storage dependencies to preserve layer discipline

body:
Follow-up from the #1297 review.

`SqlEntityStorage::mergeBundleSubtableRow()` returns silently when `bundleSubtableExists($bundle)` is false, even if the bundle has registered fields. If the schema is mid-migration (fields registered, `ensureBundleSubtable()` not yet run), `load()` returns an entity with only core values — and no runtime signal.

The `MISSING_BUNDLE_SUBTABLE` diagnostic catches this on `health:check`, but the gap between "entity loaded without bundle fields" and "operator runs `health:check`" can be long.

**Design call needed:**

- **Log warning** — low friction, matches write-path drift logging. Preferred by review: shortens feedback loop without destabilizing reads.
- **Throw** — safer but breaks the "open by default, diagnostic-driven" operational model and would cascade into every caller of `load()`.
- **No-op (current)** — relies on operator discipline.

**Recommendation from review:** log a warning once per `(entity_type, bundle)` via `$this->logger->warning(...)`, similar to how `mapRowToEntity()` logs JSON drift. Memoize on the already-cached `bundleSubtableCache` to avoid log flooding on bulk loads.

**Spec:** `docs/specs/bundle-scoped-storage.md`.
title: Log warning when load() encounters a registered-field bundle with no subtable

body:
Follow-up from the #1297 review.

`SqlSchemaHandler::bundleSubtableName()` validates that a bundle id does not contain `__` (the reserved separator) and throws `\InvalidArgumentException` on violation. `SqlEntityStorage::bundleSubtableName()` (private) and `SqlEntityQuery::resolveField()` / the bundle JOIN loop both construct the same `{base}__{bundle}` name via raw string concatenation without the guard.

Result: a bundle id containing `__` would fail-loud at schema creation but silently mistarget at read/write paths — divergent invariants.

**Options:**

1. **Extract a shared helper** — static on `SqlSchemaHandler` or a small `BundleSubtable` value object — that storage, query, and diagnostics all call.
2. **Enforce the guard at bundle registration time** — `EntityTypeManager::addBundleFields()` or `GroupType` config validation — so no downstream component needs to re-validate.

Option 2 is stronger: the invariant becomes structural rather than re-enforced at each name-construction site.

**Spec:** `docs/specs/bundle-scoped-storage.md` §Naming.
title: Centralize bundle-subtable name helper with `__` separator guard

## Context

\`AuthorizeController::__invoke()\` builds the anonymous-login redirect as:

\`\`\`
\$this->loginPath . '?return_to=' . rawurlencode(\$request->getRequestUri())
\`\`\`

If \`config['oidc']['login_path']\` is configured with a pre-existing query string (e.g. \`/login?foo=bar\`), the result is \`/login?foo=bar?return_to=...\` — malformed.

Default \`/login\` is unaffected.

## Fix

Use the same \`appendQuery()\` helper the controller already has for building the success/error redirect URLs.

## Priority

Low — only triggers for non-default \`login_path\` configurations.

## Context

`PackageManifestCompiler` already reads `extra.waaseyaa.migrations` from each package's composer.json, and `MigrationLoader::loadAll()` walks the manifest's migrations map. But no framework package currently declares a migrations path, and there's no end-to-end example of a package shipping a migration.

Practical symptom: packages that need additional entity-type columns beyond the base (id/uuid/langcode/_data) have nowhere to put schema definitions. Current workaround (e.g. waaseyaa/oidc, PR #1285) is to call \`SqlSchemaHandler::addFieldColumns()\` idempotently in \`ServiceProvider::boot()\`. It works, but:

- It runs on every boot
- It duplicates the schema knowledge between the entity type's field definitions and the provider's boot logic
- It couples schema evolution to code paths rather than migration history

## Scope

- Establish the convention for a package shipping migrations (path layout, naming, \`extra.waaseyaa.migrations\` entry in composer.json)
- Verify the package-migration discovery path end-to-end via an existing package (candidate: waaseyaa/oidc, since it has an immediate use case)
- Document the pattern in a spec (likely \`docs/specs/infrastructure.md\` or a new migration-focused spec)
- Remove the boot-time \`addFieldColumns\` workaround from \`OidcServiceProvider::boot()\` once the oidc package has a proper migration

## Non-goals

- Replacing the kernel's auto \`ensureTable()\` behavior (that handles base entity columns)
- Building a migration UI/admin surface

## Acceptance

- At least one framework package ships a migration via \`extra.waaseyaa.migrations\`
- \`waaseyaa migrate:up\` runs it cleanly on a fresh install
- Spec documents the convention
- \`OidcServiceProvider::boot()\` no longer calls \`addFieldColumns\` (follow-up PR after this lands)

## Related

- Unblocks cleanup of PR #1285 boot-time workaround
- Referenced by #1275 (ADR-004 follow-up on \`replace\`'d packages and discovery)

## Context

The 2026-04-16 release-pipeline incident (origin/main force-pushed down to a single composer.json) was caused by `split.yml` publishing a matrix entry whose `remote: framework` resolved to this monorepo itself. The token used for that push (`SPLIT_GITHUB_TOKEN`) had write access to this repo.

Defence-in-depth layers now in place (as of v0.1.0-alpha.145):

1. `split.yml` self-split guard (commit 8ba58d34d) rejects any matrix entry whose `remote` equals the current repo name.
2. `verify-monorepo-main-intact` job blocks release publication if origin SHA drifted.
3. Branch protection ruleset on `main` (ruleset id 15181711) blocks `non_fast_forward` and `deletion` for all actors including Actions. Bypass actors: none.
4. ADR-004 (partial): `packages/framework` metapackage deleted — the collision artifact no longer exists in the tree.

The token is now one of four concentric guards. Rotating it to a fine-grained PAT that excludes `waaseyaa/framework` makes it impossible for this token to touch the monorepo, regardless of future workflow changes.

## Proposal

1. Create fine-grained PAT scoped to every `waaseyaa/*` split target listed in `.github/workflows/split.yml` **except** `waaseyaa/framework`. Permissions: `contents: write` on each listed repo only.
2. Replace the `SPLIT_GITHUB_TOKEN` repo secret.
3. Revoke the old token.

## Scope

Owner-operated (credential management). Not blocking other work.

body:
## Context

[ADR-004](docs/adr/004-framework-package-collapse.md) was executed partially in v0.1.0-alpha.145 (commit 2b2ee324d): `packages/framework` metapackage is deleted and the primary invariant (ADR-004 §2: exactly one authoritative `waaseyaa/framework` composer.json, at the repo root) is enforced.

The secondary goal — rewriting the root `composer.json` to `type: library` with a `replace` block (ADR-004 §5) — was deferred. `replace` semantics empty `vendor/composer/installed.json` of first-party entries, and framework discovery paths read from that file. Running the ADR-004 §5 rewrite caused 11 test failures and 2 PHPStan errors.

## Discovery paths to fix

**Confirmed breakage:**

- `packages/foundation/src/Discovery/PackageManifestCompiler.php:52` — reads `vendor/composer/installed.json` and iterates packages for `extra.waaseyaa.providers`, `.commands`, `.routes`, `.migrations`, `.permissions`.

**Likely also affected (not fully audited in the deferral session):**

- `Waaseyaa\Foundation\Kernel\Bootstrap\ProviderRegistry`
- Migration loader
- Classmap scanning (line 396 in `PackageManifestCompiler`)
- Any other `installed.json` consumer

## Proposal

Teach these discovery paths to synthesize missing entries from the root `composer.json`'s `replace` block + path repositories.

Algorithm sketch: for each name in `replace`, look up its `type: path` repository entry in `composer.json`, read `{path}/composer.json`, derive the same metadata that `installed.json` would have carried (`extra.waaseyaa.*` and classmap paths).

Once discovery is robust, revisit ADR-004 §5 and land the `type: library` + `replace` rewrite as a single release.

## References

- ADR: `docs/adr/004-framework-package-collapse.md`
- Implementation plan (with Status notes on partial execution): `docs/plans/2026-04-16-framework-package-collapse-implementation.md`
- Partial execution commit: 2b2ee324d
- Failed tests when `replace` was in place: 8 × `SsrHttpKernelIntegrationTest` + 2 × `PackageManifestCompilerTest` + 1 × `ProviderDrivenRouteRegistrationTest`

## Not blocking

ADR-004's primary invariant (single `waaseyaa/framework` composer.json) is already enforced. The `replace` upgrade is a nice-to-have (stricter bundled version semantics). Defence-in-depth against the original incident is already in place: self-split guard, `verify-monorepo-main-intact` job, branch protection ruleset, metapackage deletion.
title: ADR-004 follow-up: teach package discovery to synthesize installed.json entries for `replace`d packages

## Status

Implementation lives in #1264 — `waaseyaa/northcloud` package, ready for review.

This issue tracks the multi-phase rollout that spans beyond that PR.

## Why the package exists

North Cloud content consumption was duplicated across apps:

| App | Current integration |
|---|---|
| **Minoo** | Full client + sync service + worker + search provider + mappers (~720 LOC of in-tree `App\Support\`, `App\Ingestion\`, `App\Search\`) |
| **Giiken** | Adopting via #1264 — one concrete mapper (`NcHitToKnowledgeItemMapper`), no framework-level wiring needed |
| **Future apps** | Would otherwise copy-paste Minoo's code |

Each new consumer re-inventing the HTTP client, cache, sync orchestrator, and search provider is a copy-paste-diverge trap. Extracting into `waaseyaa/northcloud` leaves only concrete entity mappers in the app.

## Rollout phases

- [x] **v0.1.0-alpha.1** — package scaffold, client, cache, sync service, search provider, `northcloud:sync` command, mapper registry. (#1264)
- [x] **Giiken adopts first** — proves the abstraction against a real consumer. End-to-end dry-run against `api.northcloud.one` succeeds: fetch → map → skip/persist.
- [ ] **v0.1.0-alpha.2 — Minoo migration.** The real test. Minoo's `App\Support\NorthCloudClient`, `App\Ingestion\NcContentSyncService`, `App\Search\NorthCloudSearchProvider`, `App\Support\Command\NcSyncCommand` get deleted and replaced by the package + four concrete `NcHitToEntityMapperInterface` implementations. ~720 LOC of app code should vanish. Any shape issues surface here before API stability.
- [ ] **v0.1.0 (stable)** — after Minoo migration lands cleanly. Tag on Packagist, let other consumers require a stable version.

## Known follow-ups (not blocking alpha)

- Foundation config auto-merging: #1264 works around unmerged package configs by env fallback in `NorthCloudServiceProvider::northcloudConfig()`. Longer-term, the foundation should auto-merge `config/*.php` from packages with `extra.waaseyaa.providers`.
- `bin-dir` collision: `waaseyaa/cli` declares `bin/waaseyaa`, which Composer writes into consumer `./bin/` and clobbers hand-written wrappers. Giiken worked around by renaming its wrapper to `bin/giiken`. Tracked upstream as #1226.

## References

- Package: `packages/northcloud/` (after #1264 merges)
- Minoo's in-tree implementation: `~/dev/minoo/src/Support/NorthCloudClient.php`, `~/dev/minoo/src/Ingestion/NcContentSyncService.php`, `~/dev/minoo/src/Search/NorthCloudSearchProvider.php`
- North Cloud backend: `~/dev/north-cloud`
- Laravel-side equivalent (different runtime): `~/dev/northcloud-laravel`

body:
## Problem

Entity query conditions on `_data` JSON fields fail when the PHP value is a string but the JSON stores an integer.

SQLite's `json_extract()` returns native integer type for JSON integer values. PDO binds PHP strings as TEXT. SQLite type comparison rules treat INTEGER and TEXT as unequal even when they represent the same number — so `json_extract(_data, '$.user_id') = '13'` returns no rows when the stored value is `13` (integer).

## Reproduction

```php
// Returns [1] — integer binding works
$storage->getQuery()->condition('user_id', 13)->execute();

// Returns [] — string binding fails
$storage->getQuery()->condition('user_id', '13')->execute();
```

Verified directly in SQLite:
```sql
SELECT fid FROM follow WHERE json_extract(_data, '$.user_id') = 13;    -- returns row
SELECT fid FROM follow WHERE json_extract(_data, '$.user_id') = '13';  -- returns nothing
```

## Root cause

The entity query builder does not cast values before binding them. `AccountInterface::id()` returns `string|int`, and calling code that passes the string form silently gets empty results.

## Fix

In the entity query `condition()` builder, detect numeric string values and bind them as integers when the target field is stored in `_data` JSON. Alternatively, generate `CAST(json_extract(...) AS TEXT)` so comparisons are always text-to-text.

## Workaround (applied in Minoo)

Cast to `(int)` at the call site: `->condition('user_id', (int) $account->id())`

This is fragile — callers should not need to know the internal storage type.
title: fix(entity-query): _data integer fields fail string comparisons in SQLite

## Context

The admin SPA catch-all route (#1246) calls `file_get_contents()` on every `/admin/*` request to serve `public/admin/index.html`. Fine for dev, suboptimal for production.

## Options

1. **Response caching** — read once, store in static var for process lifetime
2. **X-Sendfile** — let nginx/Apache serve the file directly
3. **BinaryFileResponse** — Symfony's response with `If-Modified-Since`, `Content-Length` handling

Low priority — file is small and PHP process lifetime is short.

Ref: #1246

## Problem

`phpstan.neon` references `phpstan-strict-rules/rules.neon`, and `phpstan/phpstan-strict-rules` is listed in root `composer.json` require-dev, but `composer.lock` doesn't include it. Running `composer phpstan` fails:

```
File 'vendor/phpstan/phpstan-strict-rules/rules.neon' is missing or is not readable.
```

## Fix

```bash
composer update phpstan/phpstan-strict-rules
```

Then commit the updated `composer.lock`.

## Context

The admin SPA catch-all controller closure in `AdminSurfaceServiceProvider` (added in #1246) reads `public/admin/index.html` via `file_get_contents()` inline. The test for the "serves index.html" path only tests PHP filesystem APIs, not the actual controller.

## Proposal

Extract the serve-or-fallback logic into a static method on `AdminSpaFallback`:

```php
public static function serveOrFallback(string $projectRoot): Response
```

Then the controller closure delegates to this method, tests can call it directly, and production optimization has a single entry point.

Also: the `adminSpaFallbackIsReturnedWhenIndexHtmlMissing` test creates a temp directory but never uses it — clean up the dead setup/teardown.

Ref: #1246

## Problem

Five classes in the Interface layer (Layer 6) are `final` without interfaces. These are registries and central handlers that apps need to extend or substitute for custom UI, command discovery, and rendering behaviour.

**Audit reference:** `docs/audits/final-keyword-audit.md` (2026-04-12)

## Affected Classes

### 1. `CliCommandRegistry` (`packages/cli/src/CliCommandRegistry.php`)
- Registry with no interface; apps cannot substitute command discovery

### 2. `GraphQlEndpoint` (`packages/graphql/src/GraphQlEndpoint.php`)
- Core endpoint with heavy DI; apps cannot substitute for custom GraphQL execution

### 3. `ComponentRegistry` (`packages/ssr/src/ComponentRegistry.php`)
- Apps adding custom SSR components cannot extend registration

### 4. `FieldFormatterRegistry` (`packages/ssr/src/FieldFormatterRegistry.php`)
- Hard-registers formatters; apps cannot add custom field formatters without replacing the class

### 5. `SsrPageHandler` (`packages/ssr/src/SsrPageHandler.php`)

2. Keep `final` on all implementations

- [ ] All service provider bindings use interface type-hints
- [ ] All consumer constructor type-hints updated
- [ ] `composer phpstan` passes

## Problem

Three core data services in Layer 1 are `final` without interfaces, blocking substitution for testing and app customization.

**Audit reference:** `docs/audits/final-keyword-audit.md` (2026-04-12)

## Affected Classes

### 1. `AccessChecker` (`packages/access/src/AccessChecker.php`)
- Central route-level access enforcement with constructor DI
- Tested by the routing package (`packages/routing/tests/Unit/AccessCheckerTest.php`)
- Consumers cannot substitute access-checking logic (e.g. tenant-scoped access, feature flags)

### 2. `AuthManager` (`packages/auth/src/AuthManager.php`)
- Session-manipulating auth service, tested (`AuthManagerTest.php`)
- Tests cannot substitute session behaviour

### 3. `TwoFactorManager` (`packages/auth/src/TwoFactorManager.php`)
- Pure TOTP service using `time()` internally, no clock injection
- Impossible to test time-window edge cases without subclassing

## Recommended Change

For each class:
1. Extract an interface from the public API (e.g. `AccessCheckerInterface`, `AuthManagerInterface`, `TwoFactorManagerInterface`)
2. Keep `final` on the implementation
3. Update service provider bindings to use the interface
4. Update all constructor type-hints that reference the concrete class to use the interface
5. For `TwoFactorManager`: also inject `EntityClockInterface` to replace `time()` calls

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `AccessCheckerInterface` exists and `AccessChecker` implements it
- [ ] `AuthManagerInterface` exists and `AuthManager` implements it
- [ ] `TwoFactorManagerInterface` exists and `TwoFactorManager` implements it
- [ ] `TwoFactorManager` uses injected clock instead of `time()`
- [ ] All service provider bindings use interface type-hints
- [ ] All consumer constructor type-hints updated
- [ ] `composer phpstan` passes
- [ ] `./vendor/bin/phpunit` passes
title: refactor: extract interfaces for Core Data final classes
Inertia: `RootTemplateRenderer` ships `data-page="true"` but client reader expects `data-page="app"`

### Summary
Now that #1181 (casting + hydration architecture) is complete, we should enforce the
layering boundaries defined in `CLAUDE.md` using an automated layer/import checker.

This prevents future regressions such as:
- API layer reaching into entity-storage
- AI/vector layer reaching into raw entity internals
- admin-SPA schema layer reaching into storage
- circular dependencies between packages
- cross-layer imports that violate the architecture

### Goals
- Introduce a `.layers` or `.imports` policy file at the repo root.
- Define allowed → allowed import edges based on the layering table.
- Add a CI job that fails on violations.
- Provide a CLI command to run the check locally.
- Keep the implementation small and mechanical (no runtime changes).

### Non-goals
- Refactoring existing packages (ST-10 already handled functional fixes).
- Changing the layering model defined in `CLAUDE.md`.

### Acceptance criteria
- A root-level policy file describing allowed imports.
- A checker script (PHP or Node) that:
  - scans `composer.json` and PHP namespaces
  - detects forbidden edges
  - detects circular dependencies
- CI job added to GitHub Actions.
- Documentation added to `docs/infrastructure.md` or a new `docs/layers.md`.

### Notes
This is a follow-up to #1181 but intentionally separate so the milestone remains clean.
title: Layer Gate: Enforce package-level import and dependency rules

body:
## Problem

App-level code in Waaseyaa sites currently imports Symfony classes directly, leaking framework internals and hurting DX:

```php
use Symfony\Component\HttpFoundation\JsonResponse;
use Symfony\Component\HttpFoundation\Request;
use Symfony\Contracts\EventDispatcher\EventDispatcherInterface;
```

Developers building on Waaseyaa shouldn't need to know (or care) that Symfony is the underlying engine. This is the same philosophy that Laravel follows with its facades and contracts.

## Proposed Abstractions

| App code currently uses | Waaseyaa should provide |
|---|---|
| `Symfony\Component\HttpFoundation\JsonResponse` | `Waaseyaa\Api\JsonApiResponse` (wraps JsonResponse with JSON:API envelope) |
| `Symfony\Component\HttpFoundation\Request` | `Waaseyaa\Foundation\Http\Request` (re-export or thin wrapper) |
| `Symfony\Contracts\EventDispatcher\EventDispatcherInterface` | `Waaseyaa\Foundation\Event\EventDispatcherInterface` |
| Manual `new JsonResponse(['jsonapi' => ['version' => '1.1'], ...])` | `JsonApiResponseTrait` (started in alpha.104, expand usage) |

## Context

The northops-waaseyaa Lead Intelligence Pipeline PR (jonesrussell/northops-waaseyaa#127) surfaced this across 6+ files. Every controller constructs raw Symfony JsonResponse objects with repeated JSON:API envelope boilerplate.

The `JsonApiResponseTrait` added in alpha.104 is a good start. The next step is making it the standard pattern and providing a clean response builder that app developers reach for instead of `new JsonResponse(...)`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] App controllers can return responses without importing Symfony
- [ ] Event dispatching available via a Waaseyaa interface
- [ ] JSON:API envelope construction handled by framework helpers
- [ ] Existing apps continue to work (Symfony imports still function, just not recommended)
title: feat: wrap Symfony dependencies so app code never imports Symfony directly

body:
## Problem

`AccountInterface` mixes two accessor naming conventions:

```php
public function id(): int|string;          // no prefix
public function getRoles(): array;         // get prefix
public function hasPermission(): bool;     // has prefix
public function isAuthenticated(): bool;   // is prefix
```

This creates friction when implementing the interface and when reading code that calls it. `id()` in particular is a surprise — every other accessor follows a verb prefix pattern.

## Impact

- Implementors of `AccountInterface` have to remember which methods use `get` vs no prefix
- Code that calls `$account->id()` reads ambiguously — it looks like a property access rather than a method call
- The inconsistency will propagate to any other `*Interface` that follows the same pattern

## Proposed resolution

Audit all public interfaces in the framework for accessor naming consistency. At minimum, decide and document one of:

1. **All getters use `get` prefix** — rename `id()` → `getId()`, align everything else
2. **No prefix for simple value returns** — drop `get` from `getRoles()` → `roles()`, `getLabel()` → `label()`, etc.
3. **PHP 8.x convention** — use no-prefix for most, reserve `get`/`is`/`has` for semantically meaningful distinctions

Whichever convention is chosen, document it in the framework coding standards and apply it consistently across all interfaces. A codemods/search pass is likely needed.

## Discovered via

[waaseyaa/giiken](https://github.com/waaseyaa/giiken) — hit this when implementing `KnowledgeItemAccessPolicy` and writing test doubles for `AccountInterface`.
title: Inconsistent accessor naming on AccountInterface: id() vs getRoles()

body:
## Context

We ran a codified context A/B test on the ddev/ddev codebase to measure whether machine-readable subsystem specs improve AI agent performance on architectural tasks. The experiment framework, scoring, and initial results live at:

- **Concept:** https://github.com/jonesrussell/ddev-context
- **Experiment framework:** `jonesrussell/ddev` branch `20260403_jonesrussell_codified_context_experiment` under `docs/experiments/codified-context-ab/`

## What to do

Once the DDEV experiment has enough data (multiple runs, model matrix results):

1. **Create codified context specs** for waaseyaa/framework's core subsystems
2. **Adapt the A/B test framework** (setup.sh, score.sh, task prompt, answer key) to this codebase
3. **Run the experiment** and compare results across both codebases
4. **Compare findings** to see if codified context helps more or less on a different architecture

## Why

Testing on a single codebase (DDEV) doesn't tell us if the approach generalizes. A second codebase with different architecture gives stronger signal about whether codified context is broadly useful or specific to certain project structures.

## Depends on

- DDEV experiment results being complete and analyzed first
title: Repeat codified context A/B test experiment for waaseyaa/framework

## Summary

`PackageManifestCompiler::load()` could return a valid `packages.php` that omitted root `extra.waaseyaa` declarations or vendor metadata after `composer.json` / `installed.json` changed, without tripping `StaleManifestException` (which targets missing provider *classes*).

## Resolution (implemented)

- Store `_manifest_inputs_fp` (`xxh128` of raw `composer.json` + `installed.json`) in the cached array; on load, mismatch triggers full `compileAndCache()`.
- Legacy caches without the fingerprint key are unchanged: no auto-recompile before provider validation.
- After a cache hit, always merge root `extra.waaseyaa` providers/commands/routes/permissions into the deserialized manifest (append-missing for lists, overwrite for permission keys).

## Spec

## Context

Claudriel v2.8 (#549) needs a CI/deploy webhook receiver with HMAC signature verification and payload routing. This is a reusable framework concern, not app-specific.

Laravel has webhook verification middleware. Drupal has the Webhooks module. Waaseyaa has neither.

## Scope

- `WebhookHandlerInterface` — process verified webhook payload
- `WebhookSignatureVerifier` — HMAC-SHA256/SHA1 signature validation (GitHub, Stripe, generic)
- `WebhookRouteMiddleware` — verify signature before hitting controller
- `WebhookPayloadNormalizer` — extract common fields (event type, timestamp, payload) from provider-specific formats
- Provider adapters: GitHub (webhook events), generic HMAC

## Why framework-level

Multiple waaseyaa apps will need webhooks:
- Claudriel: GitHub Actions CI events, deploy events
- northcloud-laravel consumers: could migrate to waaseyaa webhooks
- Any future waaseyaa app with third-party integrations

## Depends on

- waaseyaa/routing (middleware pipeline)
- waaseyaa/api (controller resolution)

## Claudriel dependency

Claudriel #549 (CI/deploy webhook receiver) depends on this.
title: feat: webhook handling abstraction (signature verification + payload routing)

body:
## Overview

Extend the existing `waaseyaa/ai-agent` package with agentic behavior patterns. The brainstem gains new capabilities while staying lean.

Parent epic: #619

## Extensions

### Planning (Pattern 6)
- `PlannerInterface` — decompose goal into ordered steps with acceptance criteria
- `PlanExecutor` — execute steps sequentially with learn-forward context
- `PlanStep` — value object: title, description, acceptance criteria, status
- Reference: Polyscope Autopilot (story generation + sequential execution + crash recovery)

### Routing (Pattern 2)
- `TaskClassifier` — categorize incoming requests by complexity/type
- `ModelRouter` — select model based on task classification
- `PromptRouter` — different system prompts for different request categories
- `FallbackChain` — if routed model fails, escalate to more capable model

### Reflection (Pattern 4)
- `ReflectionLoop` — generate, critique, refine cycle
- `SynthesisOutput` — agreement + divergence + recommendation format
- `CritiqueAgent` — second-pass review (can use different/cheaper model)
- Reference: Polyscope Opinions (multi-model parallel analysis + synthesis)

### Multi-Agent (Pattern 7)
- `AgentRegistry` — register specialist agents with scoped tools and prompts
- `Orchestrator` — delegate sub-tasks to specialists based on request type
- `ResultSynthesizer` — combine specialist outputs into coherent response
- `PeerDiscovery` — agents discover each other's capabilities
- Reference: claude-peers-mcp (broker + registry + MCP, no central controller)

### Parallelization (Pattern 3)
- `ParallelExecutor` — fan-out independent tool calls, fan-in results
- `TaskSplitter` — identify independent sub-tasks from a request
- Reference: Polyscope copy-on-write isolation

### Context Assembly (Context Engineering)
- `ContextAssembler` — selective context curation per request
- `ContextCompressor` — summarize/structure context to fit window
- `SourceSelector` — decide which knowledge sources to query
- Reference: LlamaIndex context engineering (selective retrieval, not dump-everything)

## Dependencies

- `waaseyaa/ai-memory` — for planning persistence, reflection context
- `waaseyaa/ai-guardrails` — for tool permission checks during execution
- `waaseyaa/ai-observability` — for trace recording during execution
- `waaseyaa/ai-schema` — for tool definitions
- `waaseyaa/ai-vector` — optional, for semantic routing

## Pattern Coverage

Patterns 1 (Prompt Chaining), 2 (Routing), 3 (Parallelization), 4 (Reflection), 6 (Planning), 7 (Multi-Agent), 10 (MCP), 11 (Goal Setting), 15 (A2A), 17 (Reasoning), 20 (Prioritization), 21 (Exploration)
title: feat: ai-agent kernel extensions — planning, routing, reflection, multi-agent

## Overview

New package: `waaseyaa/ai-guardrails`. The immune system of the agentic framework.

Parent epic: #619

## Responsibility

Cross-cutting safety layer. Programmatic enforcement of what agents can and cannot do, not prompts-as-policy.

## Interfaces & Components

### Permission Model
- `ToolPermissionPolicy` — tiered access per tool (read-only / write-low / write-high / destructive)
- `CanUseToolCallback` — programmatic check before tool execution (Polyscope pattern)
- `PermissionResolver` — resolve effective permissions from policies + context
- `ActionClassifier` — classify actions as reversible vs. irreversible

### Safety Policies
- `GuardrailPolicy` — configurable rules engine (not prompt instructions)
- `InputValidator` — sanitize external inputs, prompt injection defense
- `OutputValidator` — PII leakage detection, hallucination flags, content filtering
- `ModelConstraint` — token limits, cost caps, rate limit management

### Assurance Hooks
- `PreExecutionHook` — validate before agent acts
- `PostExecutionHook` — validate after agent acts, before presenting to user
- `EscalationHandler` — route to human when confidence is low or action is high-impact

### Audit
- `GuardrailAuditLog` — every permission decision logged with reasoning

## Dependencies

None. Pure policy layer. Any package can use it.

## Pattern Coverage

- Pattern 18 (Guardrails/Safety) — primary
- Pattern 13 (Human-in-the-Loop) — escalation handlers, approval gates
- Trifecta "Assurance" dimension — the lever that unlocks progressive autonomy

## References

- Polyscope: canUseTool callback, directory jailing, read-only mode, plan mode
- Autonomous AI Trifecta: permissions as product surface, tiered privileges
- Claudriel issues: #538 (guardrails)
title: feat: ai-guardrails package — permissions, safety, and assurance

## Overview

New package: `waaseyaa/ai-memory`. The hippocampus of the agentic framework.

Parent epic: #619

## Responsibility

All forms of agent memory: conversation history, semantic knowledge, episodic recall, and the backward-propagation patterns that keep memory alive.

## Interfaces & Components

### Memory Stores
- `ConversationMemoryStore` — session/thread management, message history
- `SemanticMemoryStore` — fact extraction, long-term knowledge (depends on ai-vector)
- `EpisodicMemoryStore` — event-based recall with temporal windows
- `StaticMemoryBlock` — fixed context (user preferences, personality)

### Memory Operations
- `MemorySummarizer` — compress conversation history for context windows
- `MemoryRetriever` — semantic search across memory stores, ranked results
- `MemoryConsolidator` — merge duplicate/overlapping memories
- `MemoryDecay` — temporal relevance scoring, fade stale memories
- `ReweaveProcessor` — backward context propagation (Ars Contexta pattern)
- `FactExtractor` — extract discrete facts from conversations

### Memory Entities
- `Memory` — base memory entity type (fact, episode, conversation summary)
- `MemorySession` — conversation session with thread tracking
- `MemoryTurn` — individual message in a session

## Dependencies

- `waaseyaa/ai-vector` — for semantic memory embeddings and similarity search
- `waaseyaa/entity` — for memory entity persistence
- `waaseyaa/entity-storage` — for SQL storage

Does NOT depend on `waaseyaa/ai-agent`.

## Pattern Coverage

- Pattern 8 (Memory Management) — primary
- Pattern 14 (RAG / Knowledge Retrieval) — semantic memory + vector search
- Pattern 9 (Learning & Adaptation) — fact extraction + memory evolution
- Context Engineering — memory types (Vector, FactExtraction, Static)

## References

- LlamaIndex memory types: VectorMemoryBlock, FactExtractionMemoryBlock, StaticMemoryBlock
- Ars Contexta 6 Rs: Reweave (backward pass), Rethink (challenge assumptions)
- Claudriel issues: #524 (memory), #525 (RAG)

body:
## Overview

Extend waaseyaa into a native agentic framework by adding three strategic organ packages alongside the existing AI packages. The brainstem (ai-agent) stays lean and orchestrates; the organs (memory, guardrails, observability) provide independent capabilities.

**RFC:** `claudriel/docs/specs/waaseyaa-agentic-framework-rfc.md`
**Claudriel epic:** jonesrussell/claudriel#523

## Architecture

```
ai-schema <--- ai-agent ---> ai-memory
ai-vector <-------+    +---> ai-guardrails
ai-pipeline <-----+    +---> ai-observability
```

Organs don't depend on the brainstem. The brainstem orchestrates them all.

## Package Inventory

### Existing (extend)
- [x] **ai-agent** — agent loop, tool registry, MCP server, providers
- [x] **ai-schema** — JSON Schema + MCP tool generation (stable)
- [x] **ai-pipeline** — step-based processing + queues (stable)
- [x] **ai-vector** — embeddings + vector stores (stable)

### New (build)
- [ ] **ai-memory** — conversation + semantic memory, reweave, compression, retrieval
- [ ] **ai-guardrails** — permissions, tool safety, input/output validation, assurance hooks
- [ ] **ai-observability** — traces, cost tracking, metrics, anomaly detection, introspection

### Extend ai-agent kernel
- [ ] Planning: goal decomposition, step execution, learn-forward context
- [ ] Routing: task classification, dynamic model selection
- [ ] Reflection: self-critique loop, synthesis output
- [ ] Multi-agent: agent registry, delegation, result synthesis
- [ ] Parallelization: fan-out/fan-in for independent tool calls
- [ ] Context assembly: selective context curation per request

## Design Principles

1. Organs don't depend on the brainstem
2. Entity system is the persistence layer
3. Extract patterns from LangGraph/CrewAI/Neuron AI, build waaseyaa-native
4. Composable via service providers
5. Zero downstream knowledge (waaseyaa doesn't know about Claudriel)

## Pattern Coverage

All 21 agentic design patterns from Gullí's framework covered across the 7 packages. See RFC for full mapping.
title: epic: Waaseyaa Native Agentic Framework

body:
## Audit Reference
Audit §12 — In-memory array pagination. Memory pressure on large graphs.

## Description
`RelationshipDiscoveryService` uses `array_slice()` on in-memory arrays with defaults of 20/12 items. For large relationship graphs, this loads all edges into memory before slicing.

Replace with SQL-level LIMIT/OFFSET pagination:
1. Push pagination to the database query
2. Only load the requested page of results
3. Return total count separately (COUNT query)

**File:** `packages/relationship/src/RelationshipDiscoveryService.php`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Pagination pushed to SQL LIMIT/OFFSET
- [ ] Memory usage constant regardless of total relationship count
- [ ] Total count available without loading all rows
- [ ] Existing API contract preserved (same response shape)
title: perf: replace in-memory array pagination in RelationshipDiscoveryService

body:
## Audit Reference
Audit §12 — No dataloader pattern. GraphQL: N+1 for every nested field.

## Description
GraphQL nested field resolution triggers individual entity loads. A query requesting 20 nodes with their authors triggers 20 author loads.

Implement a DataLoader-style batch resolver:
1. Collect all entity IDs requested during a GraphQL resolution pass
2. Batch-load at the end of each resolution level
3. Distribute results to waiting resolvers

**File:** `packages/graphql/` layer

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `EntityDataLoader` class that batches loads per entity type
- [ ] GraphQL resolvers use DataLoader instead of direct repository calls
- [ ] Batch loads occur at resolution level boundaries
- [ ] Integration test: nested query produces O(depth) queries, not O(nodes)
title: perf: add DataLoader pattern for GraphQL nested field resolution

body:
## Audit Reference
Audit §12 — No query result caching. Repeated identical queries not cached.

## Description
`SqlEntityQuery` executes identical SQL on every call with no caching layer. Within a single request, the same entity can be loaded multiple times.

Implement per-request identity map:
1. Cache loaded entities by type + ID in memory
2. Return cached entity on subsequent loads within same request
3. Invalidate on save/delete

**File:** `packages/entity-storage/src/SqlEntityQuery.php`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Identity map caches entities per request
- [ ] Second `find($id)` returns cached instance (no SQL)
- [ ] Cache invalidated on `save()` and `delete()`
- [ ] Cache cleared between requests (no stale data)
- [ ] Unit test verifying cache hit/miss behavior
title: perf: add query result caching to SqlEntityQuery

body:
## Audit Reference
Audit §12 — No eager loading. Every relation = separate query.

## Description
`EntityRepository` has no mechanism to pre-load related entities. Each entity reference field triggers a separate query on access.

Add `findBy()` option to specify eager-loaded relations:
```php
$repository->findBy(['status' => 'published'], include: ['author', 'tags']);
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `include` parameter on `findBy()` and `find()`
- [ ] Related entities loaded in batch queries (not N+1)
- [ ] Included entities hydrated and attached to parent entity
- [ ] Integration test comparing query counts with/without eager loading
title: perf: add eager loading support to EntityRepository

body:
## Audit Reference
Audit §12 — N+1 queries in entity references. Scales poorly with nested includes.

## Description
EntityReferenceItem resolution triggers a separate query per referenced entity. When serializing a list of 50 articles each with an author reference, this produces 50+ individual SELECT queries.

Implement batch loading for entity references:
1. Collect all referenced entity IDs during serialization
2. Batch-load in a single query
3. Distribute loaded entities to their referencing fields

**Location:** EntityReferenceItem resolution, ResourceSerializer includes

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Entity references batch-loaded in a single query per entity type
- [ ] Number of queries scales with entity types, not entity count
- [ ] JSON:API `?include=author` on 50-item collection produces ≤5 queries (not 50+)
- [ ] Integration test verifying query count
title: perf: fix N+1 queries in EntityReferenceItem resolution
